message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending tasks, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

'./.claude/progress.txt' is:
- your persistent memory.
- Important errors must be registered in order to avoid expending tokens again.
- Never run tasks not registered in it.
- Register new tasks if needed, respecting the current structure.


WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project Facts from CLAUDE.md
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   a. Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   b. Execute task (respecting current project structure and ./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)
   c. Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. IF THE COMMIT FAILS (pre-commit hook / lint error):
      - Add a [P0] priority task to progress.txt describing the exact errors
      - Retry the commit with `git commit --no-verify` to save your work
      - The NEXT iteration will fix the lint errors as its top priority
   i. Output status block (see below)
   j. Stop

- Nenhuma tarefa deve ser considerada completa sem testar com Playwright MVP 
  e tirar screenshots para visualização e visualizar.
  
OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed
6. For @semparar/* packages, read the real js code because some types are not updated correctly.
7. Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
8. Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)

   959→  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
   960→  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
   961→  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
   962→  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
   963→  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
   964→  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).

   966→  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
   967→  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
   968→  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
   969→  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

   975→  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)

   979→  retornam false")

   981→  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
   982→- Must be pure function, no side effects
   983→- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→
    12→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    13→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    15→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    16→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    17→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    18→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    19→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    20→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    21→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    22→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    23→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## Introduction
    28→
    29→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    30→
    31→Key decisions:
    32→
    33→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    34→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    35→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    36→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    41→
    42→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    43→
    44→```
    45→screens/Home/
    46→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    47→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    48→└── Models/
    49→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    50→```
    51→
    52→**Models/index.d.ts**
    53→
    54→```ts
    55→export interface HomeParams {
    56→  welcome?: string;
    57→}
    58→export interface HomeState {
    59→  greeting: string;
    60→  isLoading: boolean;
    61→}
    62→export interface HomeHandlers {
    63→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    64→}
    65→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    66→```
    67→
    68→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    69→
    70→```ts
    71→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from 'react';
    72→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from './Models';
    73→import { useUserStore } from '@/services/user/store'; // Zustand slice example
    74→
    75→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    76→  const name = useUserStore(s => s.name);
    77→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    78→
    79→  useEffect(() => {
    80→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    81→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    82→  }, []);
    83→
    84→  const greeting = useMemo(
    85→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || 'guest'}!`,
    86→    [
    87→      params?.welcome,
    88→      name,
    89→    ],
    90→  );
    91→
    92→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    93→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    94→  }, []);
    95→
    96→  return {
    97→    greeting,
    98→    isLoading,
    99→    onPressCTA,
   100→  };
   101→}
   102→```
   103→
   104→**view.tsx (render)**
   105→
   106→```tsx
   107→import React from 'react';
   108→import { View } from 'react-native';
   109→import { Text, Button } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   110→import { useHomeScreen } from './index';
   111→import type { HomeParams } from './Models';
   112→
   113→type Props = HomeParams;
   114→
   115→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   116→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   117→
   118→  if (isLoading) {
   119→    return (
   120→      <View>
   121→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   122→      </View>
   123→    );
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  return (
   127→    <View>
   128→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   129→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   130→    </View>
   131→  );
   132→}
   133→```
   134→
   135→---
   136→
   137→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   138→
   139→```
   140→atomic/atoms/Button/
   141→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   142→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   143→└── Models/
   144→    └── index.d.ts
   145→```
   146→
   147→**Models/index.d.ts**
   148→
   149→```ts
   150→export interface ButtonProps {
   151→  text: string;
   152→  disabled?: boolean;
   153→  onPress?: () => void;
   154→}
   155→export interface ButtonState {
   156→  pressed: boolean;
   157→}
   158→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   159→  handlePress: () => void;
   160→}
   161→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   162→```
   163→
   164→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   165→
   166→```ts
   167→import { useCallback, useState } from 'react';
   168→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from './Models';
   169→
   170→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   171→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   172→
   173→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   174→    setPressed(true);
   175→    props.onPress?.();
   176→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   177→  }, [props.onPress]);
   178→
   179→  return {
   180→    pressed,
   181→    handlePress,
   182→  };
   183→}
   184→```
   185→
   186→**view.tsx (render)**
   187→
   188→```tsx
   189→import React from 'react';
   190→import { Pressable, View } from 'react-native';
   191→import { Text } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
   192→import { useButton } from './index';
   193→import type { ButtonProps } from './Models';
   194→
   195→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   196→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   197→
   198→  return (
   199→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   200→      <View>
   201→        <Text>
   202→          {props.text}
   203→          {pressed ? ' •' : ''}
   204→        </Text>
   205→      </View>
   206→    </Pressable>
   207→  );
   208→}
   209→```
   210→
   211→---
   212→
   213→## Project Organization
   214→
   215→```
   216→src/
   217→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   218→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   219→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   220→│   ├── atoms/
   221→│   ├── molecules/
   222→│   └── organisms/
   223→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   224→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   225→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   226→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   227→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   228→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   229→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   230→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   231→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   232→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   233→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   234→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   235→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   236→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   237→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   238→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   239→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   240→```
   241→
   242→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## Services Architecture
   247→
   248→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   249→
   250→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   251→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   252→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   253→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   254→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   255→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   256→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   257→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   258→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   259→
   260→Principles:
   261→
   262→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   263→- No circular dependencies.
   264→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   269→
   270→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   271→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   272→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   273→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   274→
   275→**Slice example**
   276→
   277→```ts
   278→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
   279→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from '../models/store';
   280→
   281→export type AuthSlice = {
   282→  auth: AuthState;
   283→  authActions: AuthActions;
   284→};
   285→
   286→const initialAuth = {
   287→  user: null,
   288→  loading: false,
   289→  error: undefined,
   290→};
   291→
   292→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<
   293→  AuthSlice,
   294→  [],
   295→  [],
   296→  AuthSlice
   297→> = set => ({
   298→  auth: initialAuth,
   299→  authActions: {
   300→    setLoading: v =>
   301→      set(s => ({
   302→        auth: {
   303→          ...s.auth,
   304→          isLoading: v,
   305→        },
   306→      })),
   307→    setUser: v =>
   308→      set(s => ({
   309→        auth: {
   310→          ...s.auth,
   311→          user: v,
   312→        },
   313→      })),
   314→    setError: v =>
   315→      set(s => ({
   316→        auth: {
   317→          ...s.auth,
   318→          error: v,
   319→        },
   320→      })),
   321→    reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   322→  },
   323→});
   324→```
   325→
   326→**Root Store**
   327→
   328→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   329→
   330→```ts
   331→import { create } from 'zustand';
   332→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from 'zustand/middleware';
   333→import { immer } from 'zustand/middleware/immer';
   334→
   335→import {
   336→  createUserSlice,
   337→  type UserSlice,
   338→} from '@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user';
   339→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from '@semparar/mf-app-yourmf';
   340→
   341→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   342→
   343→/** Add your slice Type */
   344→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   345→
   346→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   347→  devtools(
   348→    persist(
   349→      immer((...a) => ({
   350→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   351→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   352→      })),
   353→      {
   354→        name: 'root-storage',
   355→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   356→        partialize: s => ({
   357→          user: s.user,
   358→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   359→        }),
   360→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({
   361→          ...current,
   362→          ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>),
   363→        }),
   364→      },
   365→    ),
   366→    { name: 'RootStore' },
   367→  ),
   368→);
   369→```
   370→
   371→- For Use on Class.
   372→
   373→```ts
   374→class Auth {
   375→  private readonly setAuth;
   376→
   377→  constructor () {
   378→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   379→  }
   380→
   381→  function doSomething(data) {
   382→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   383→  }
   384→}
   385→```
   386→
   387→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   388→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   389→
   390→```ts
   391→const loading = useRootStore(s => s.user.isLoading);
   392→const token = useRootStore(s => s.auth.token);
   393→```
   394→
   395→---
   396→
   397→## Routes Naming
   398→
   399→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   400→
   401→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   402→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   403→
   404→---
   405→
   406→## Folder Naming Rules
   407→
   408→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                   |
   409→| ----------------------------------- | ------------ | -------------------------- |
   410→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails` |
   411→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`   |
   412→
   413→---
   414→
   415→## Code Standards
   416→
   417→**Language**: English only.  
   418→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   419→
   420→**Explanatory variables**
   421→
   422→```js
   423→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   424→const getNumberValidation = message => value =>
   425→  !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   426→const isNumber = getNumberValidation('error message');
   427→isNumber(1);
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Semantic constants**
   431→
   432→```js
   433→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   434→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   435→```
   436→
   437→**Library workarounds with link**
   438→
   439→```js
   440→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   441→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   442→event.target.value = '';
   443→```
   444→
   445→**JSDoc**
   446→
   447→```js
   448→/**
   449→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   450→ * @param {*} eventName
   451→ * @param {*} maxLength
   452→ */
   453→```
   454→
   455→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→useEffect(() => {
   459→  (async () => {
   460→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   461→    setSum(42);
   462→  })();
   463→}, [setSum]);
   464→```
   465→
   466→**Extract conditions**
   467→
   468→```ts
   469→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   470→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   471→```
   472→
   473→**Optional chaining**
   474→
   475→```ts
   476→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   477→```
   478→
   479→**JSX indentation**
   480→
   481→```tsx
   482→{
   483→  !isEnd && <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />;
   484→}
   485→```
   486→
   487→**Early returns**
   488→
   489→```ts
   490→if (!data) return null;
   491→return <View />;
   492→```
   493→
   494→**Nullish coalescing**
   495→
   496→```ts
   497→return data ?? 0;
   498→```
   499→
   500→---
   501→
   502→## Styles Patterns
   503→
   504→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   505→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   506→
   507→---
   508→
   509→## Cache Pattern
   510→
   511→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   512→
   513→```ts
   514→import storage from '~/redux/store/storage';
   515→
   516→export interface CachedData<T> {
   517→  timestamp: number;
   518→  data: T;
   519→}
   520→
   521→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   522→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   523→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   524→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   525→}
   526→
   527→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   528→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   529→
   530→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   531→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   532→    if (!json) return null;
   533→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   534→  }
   535→
   536→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   537→    const payload: CachedData<T> = {
   538→      timestamp: Date.now(),
   539→      data,
   540→    };
   541→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   542→  }
   543→
   544→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   545→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   546→  }
   547→}
   548→```
   549→
   550→**Guidelines**
   551→
   552→- One storage class per module/feature.
   553→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.

Perfect! Now let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis of the SDUI demo pattern and the complete file structure:

## Complete Analysis: SDUI Demo Pattern & Payload Structure

### 1. **Story Files Overview**

**SDUIVoucherScreens.stories.tsx** (`/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIVoucherScreens.stories.tsx`)
- Main demo for the complete voucher flow
- Uses `ProductToggle` to switch between two product types: "Abastecimento" and "Estacionamento"
- Demonstrates 4 separate screens (Form, Summary, Conclusion, Rules) and a full flow story
- Leverages `useSDUIJsonEditor` hook for live JSON editing in Storybook addon panel
- Key exports: `Form`, `Summary`, `Conclusion`, `Rules`, `FullFlow` story variants

**SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx** (`/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`)
- Dedicated story for voucher list/card demonstration
- Shows 3 view states: list (with 6 voucher cards), empty state, and filtered empty state
- Demonstrates `voucher_card` and `empty_state` adapter usage
- All components built from JSON via ScreenRenderer
- Each voucher card shows different statuses: success, highlight, error, alert

### 2. **Mock Structure** (`src/services/sdui/mocks/`)

Four mock files export paired payloads for Abastecimento/Estacionamento products:

**voucherForm.ts** - Form screen for collecting points/reais input
- Contains: heading, balance_display, points_input (2x with separator), text, link
- Context includes: product, balance, conversion data
- Uses interpolation: `{{product.key}}`, `{{balance.pointsFormatted}}`, etc.

**voucherSummary.ts** - Review/confirmation screen
- Contains: heading, text (multiple), separator, alert
- Context includes: product, selectedPoints, selectedReais
- Displays info for review before creation

**voucherConclusion.ts** - Success screen
- Contains: icon_text_row, section_heading (centered), text, link
- Minimal context (just product)
- Confirmation message and link to vouchers

**voucherRules.ts** - Educational rules/terms screen
- Contains: heading, multiple section_heading + icon_text_row combinations
- Rich context with product, conversion, support, voucher validity data
- Heavy interpolation of dynamic values

**index.ts** - Exports all mocks

### 3. **Adapter Registry** (`src/services/sdui/adapters/`)

**index.ts** - Central registry that maps component types to adapter functions:
```
heading → HeadingAdapter
text → TextAdapter
balance_display → BalanceDisplayAdapter
points_input → PointsInputAdapter
button → ButtonAdapter
link → LinkAdapter
alert → AlertAdapter
list_text_icon → ListTextIconAdapter
separator → SeparatorAdapter
icon_text_row → IconTextRowAdapter
section_heading → SectionHeadingAdapter
rich_text → RichTextAdapter
voucher_card → VoucherCardAdapter
empty_state → EmptyStateAdapter
```

**VoucherCardAdapter.tsx** - Renders CardVehicleTags from design system
- Props: icon, vehicleName, title, titleDescription, date, dateNumber, statusTitle, status, typeTag, ghostButtonText
- Maps typeTag values (success/error/alert/highlight) to design system types

**EmptyStateAdapter.tsx** - Renders empty state UI
- Props: icon, title, description, iconColor (optional), bgColor (optional)
- Centers icon in 80x80 circle, displays title and optional description below

### 4. **SDUI Type System** (`src/services/sdui/models/index.ts`)

**Core Structures:**
- `SDUIContext` - Object containing dynamic context values (strings, numbers, objects, arrays)
- `SDUIComponent` - Has `type`, `props`, optional `visible` conditional
- `SDUIScreen` - Has `id`, `context`, optional `header`, required `body[]`, optional `footer`

**Dynamic Features:**
- **Interpolation**: `{{path.to.context}}` syntax in string props
- **Conditionals**: `{ rule: { '>': [{ var: 'form.points' }, 0] } }` for visibility/enabled state
- **Validation**: `{ rules: [{ rule: {...}, message: '...' }] }` for input validation
- **Rich Text**: `{ type: 'rich_text', children: [...] }` with text/link/icon/br nodes

**JSON Logic Subset:**
- Comparison: `==`, `!=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`
- Logical: `and`, `or`, `!`
- Variable access: `{ var: 'path.in.context' }`

### 5. **ScreenRenderer** (`src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx`)

**Rendering Flow:**
1. Separates conditional props from interpolatable props
2. Interpolates string values with context using `interpolateDeep()`
3. Evaluates visibility rules using `evaluateRule()`
4. Looks up adapter from registry using component type
5. Renders with fallback if adapter not found

**Output Structure:**
```
<View flex:1>
  {header && renderComponent(header)}
  <View flex:1>
    {body.map((comp, idx) => renderComponent(comp))}
  </View>
  {footer && renderComponent(footer)}
</View>
```

### 6. **Storybook Setup** (`.storybook/preview.tsx`)

- Decorators: PhoneFrameDecorator + ThemeProvider
- Global types: phone frame related
- Initial globals: phone frame initial state

### 7. **Key Design Patterns**

**Product Toggle Pattern** (SDUIVoucherScreens):
```tsx
{PRODUCTS.map(p => (
  <Pressable onPress={() => onSelect(p)} ...>
    <Text>{p}</Text>
  </Pressable>
))}
```

**Screen Pair Pattern**:
```tsx
const SCREEN_PAIRS: Record<string, ScreenPair> = {
  Form: { Abastecimento: screen1, Estacionamento: screen2 },
  Summary: { Abastecimento: screen3, Estacionamento: screen4 },
  // ...
}
```

**JSON Editor Integration**:
```tsx
const screen = useSDUIJsonEditor(pair[product]);
// Auto-syncs with Storybook addon panel for live editing
```

**Registration Pattern**:
```tsx
registerAllAdapters(); // Must be called once per story to populate registry
```

### 8. **File Paths Summary**

- Stories: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUI*.stories.tsx`
- Mocks: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/*.ts`
- Adapters: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/*.tsx`
- Models: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/models/index.ts`
- Renderer: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/renderer/ScreenRenderer.tsx`
- Preview: `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/.storybook/preview.tsx`

This is a well-architected SDUI implementation with separation of concerns: data (mocks), rendering logic (ScreenRenderer), component adapters (adapters), and UI demos (stories).

# Code Review: SP_0005.0 -- SDUI Demo: Meus Vouchers List

## BUG

### BUG-1: Hardcoded body repetition instead of using a repeater/loop construct

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/voucherList.ts`, lines 61-145

The `voucherListLoaded` payload manually duplicates the same `voucher_card` block 6 times, differing only in the array index (`orders.0`, `orders.1`, ... `orders.5`). This is not a runtime bug per se -- interpolation will resolve correctly -- but it represents a fundamental SDUI design smell that will become a real bug at scale.

If the backend sends a response with `N` items in `orders`, the contract requires the body to contain exactly `N` copy-pasted blocks. This couples payload size to body length, defeating one of the core SDUI value propositions (data-driven rendering). The RFC (`.claude/rfc-conteudo-dinamico.txt`) references `{{var}}` interpolation, but the current engine has no `repeat`/`each`/`for_each` directive.

**Recommendation:** This is acceptable for the spike/demo scope, but should be explicitly called out as a known limitation with a `// TODO: SP_XXXX — replace with repeat directive when available` comment at the top of the body array. Without this, a future reader will assume this is the intended pattern.

**Severity:** Not a blocking bug for a spike. Becomes a blocking architectural gap before production.

---

## ISSUE

### ISSUE-1: `nav_header` renders as FallbackComponent

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/voucherList.ts`, lines 58, 153, 173

All three payloads use `type: 'nav_header'` in `header`, but there is no `nav_header` adapter registered in `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/index.ts`. The renderer will fall through to `FallbackComponent`. This is a pre-existing gap (all other mocks do the same), but the story will visibly show a fallback where a header should be.

**Recommendation:** Either add a `NavHeaderAdapter` (even a stub) or add a comment in the story explaining why the header shows a fallback block.

---

### ISSUE-2: `RealScreenPreview` duplicates mock data instead of importing from the single source of truth

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, lines 105-112

The `orders` array in `RealScreenPreview` is a manual copy of the same data in `voucherListLoaded.context.orders`. If someone changes the mock to add a 7th voucher or rename a field, the "Real Screen" side of the comparison will silently drift.

**Recommendation:** Import `voucherListLoaded` and derive the orders from `voucherListLoaded.context.orders`:

```tsx
const orders = (voucherListLoaded.context.orders as any[]).map(o => ({
  icon: o.icon,
  desc: o.description,
  pts: o.points,
  date: o.date,
  status: o.status,
}));
```

This keeps the side-by-side truly comparable.

---

### ISSUE-3: Inline styles with hardcoded color tokens in `ViewToggle`

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, lines 52-55

```tsx
backgroundColor: selected === v ? '#6B2FBE' : '#E8E0F0',
```

Per `FACT247MM013`, styles should come from `@semparar/design-system-app`. Hardcoded hex values will break under theme changes and violate the style guide. This is a story file so it is less critical, but the `ViewToggle` is a UI component being rendered inside Storybook and could be seen as a reference pattern.

**Recommendation:** Use DS tokens (e.g., `bgColor` prop on `Box`) or at minimum add a comment that these are story-only throwaway values.

---

### ISSUE-4: Missing `ghostButtonText` and `onGhostButtonPress` in the SDUI voucher card props

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/voucherList.ts`

The `RealScreenPreview` passes `ghostButtonText="Exibir detalhes"` and `onGhostButtonPress` to `CardVehicleTags` (story file, line 135-136). The `VoucherCardAdapter` also defaults `ghostButtonText` to `'Exibir detalhes'` (adapter file, line 41). However, the mock payload does not include `ghostButtonText` in the SDUI props, relying entirely on the adapter default.

This works today, but it means the backend cannot override the button text via the payload. If the intent is for this to be server-configurable, it should be in the payload. If the intent is a hardcoded default, the current approach is fine but should be documented.

---

## NIT

### NIT-1: `STATUS_COLORS` map in `RealScreenPreview` is redundant with the context data

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, lines 96-103

The `voucherListLoaded` context already maps each order to its `tag` value (`success`, `error`, `alert`, `highlight`). The `STATUS_COLORS` lookup in `RealScreenPreview` manually re-derives the same mapping. If you adopt the fix from ISSUE-2 (deriving orders from the mock), this map becomes unnecessary -- just use `o.tag` directly.

---

### NIT-2: Unused import `Icon`

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/__stories__/SDUIDemoVoucherList.stories.tsx`, line 9

```tsx
import { Text, Box, SeparatorHorizontal, CardVehicleTags, Header, Icon } from '@semparar/design-system-app';
```

`Icon` is imported but never used in the file. Remove it.

---

### NIT-3: `SeparatorHorizontal` imported but used only once in the Comparison story

Not a problem, just noting it is only used at line 181. If the Comparison story is removed later, this import becomes dead.

---

### NIT-4: Barrel export file ordering

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/mocks/index.ts`

The exports follow the implicit ordering of Form -> Summary -> Conclusion -> Rules -> List. This matches a reasonable user flow. No action needed, just confirming the ordering is intentional and good.

---

## TEST COVERAGE GAPS

### TEST-1: VoucherCardAdapter test does not assert `typeTag` fallback behavior

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/sdui/adapters/adapters.test.tsx`, lines 343-365

The existing test only covers the happy path (all props provided). The adapter has a default `typeTag = 'highlight'` and a `TAG_TYPE_MAP` fallback (`TAG_TYPE_MAP[typeTag] ?? typeTag`). There are no tests for:
- Missing `typeTag` (should default to `'highlight'`)
- Unknown `typeTag` value (falls through to the raw string via `?? typeTag`)

### TEST-2: VoucherCardAdapter test does not verify `ghostButtonText` default

The adapter defaults `ghostButtonText = 'Exibir detalhes'`, but no test asserts this. The mock in the DS mock does render it, so adding a `queryByText('Exibir detalhes')` assertion on the default case would strengthen confidence.

### TEST-3: EmptyStateAdapter does not test custom `iconColor` or `bgColor`

The adapter accepts optional `iconColor` and `bgColor` props with defaults. Only the default paths are tested. A single test with custom values would complete coverage.

---

## GOOD

1. **Clean payload structure.** The three mock variants (loaded, empty, filtered) cover the three real states the screen will have. The `id` field is distinct per variant, making them easy to identify in logs.

2. **Context interpolation usage is correct.** The `{{orders.N.field}}` syntax matches the `interpolateString` implementation, which handles array index resolution via `parseInt` in `resolveContextPath`. The paths all resolve to leaf string values, avoiding the "object returned from interpolation" edge case.

3. **Adapter isolation.** `VoucherCardAdapter` and `EmptyStateAdapter` are well-structured: they destructure with defaults, use DS components directly, and accept `context` without using it (future-proofing for when conditionals might apply at the adapter level).

4. **Side-by-side comparison story is a high-value artifact.** Having a visual diff between the traditional implementation and the SDUI-rendered version is exactly the right deliverable for a spike. It makes the value proposition tangible to stakeholders without requiring them to understand the engine internals.

5. **Barrel export pattern in mocks is consistent** with the existing form/summary/conclusion/rules pattern.

6. **Test coverage for the adapters is strong.** 14 adapters, all tested including edge cases for conditionals on ButtonAdapter, empty/missing children on RichTextAdapter, and optional description on EmptyStateAdapter.

---

## Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BUG      | 1 (architectural, non-blocking for spike) |
| ISSUE    | 4 |
| NIT      | 4 |
| TEST GAP | 3 |

The implementation is solid for a spike/demo scope. The main architectural concern (BUG-1) is the lack of a `repeat`/`each` directive, which forces body arrays to be statically sized -- this must be resolved before the pattern goes to production. The most impactful quick wins are ISSUE-2 (single source of truth for comparison data) and NIT-2 (dead import cleanup).

# SP_0006.0 — JSON Logic Evaluator
- [DONE] Implement the JSON Logic rule evaluator described in RFC section 3.5.
  > src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts — evaluateRule() function with:
  >   var operator (via resolveContextPath), comparison ops (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=),
  >   logical ops (and, or, !), nested rule evaluation as operands.
  > Security: numeric type guard on ordering ops (>, <, >=, <=) rejects non-numbers,
  >   __proto__/constructor/prototype denylist added to resolveContextPath,
  >   MAX_DEPTH=20 recursion limit prevents stack overflow from malicious payloads.
  > ! operator handles both object and array syntax ({ "!": rule } and { "!": [rule] }).
  > 39 unit tests passing (jsonLogic.test.ts): var resolution, all 6 comparison ops,
  >   and/or/! logical ops, nested logic, edge cases (null/undefined/empty/unknown ops,
  >   non-numeric ordering, depth limit, prototype pollution guard).
  > Review fixes applied: BUG-3 (! array syntax), numeric type guards, depth limit,
  >   prototype pollution denylist, COMPARISON_OPS hoisted to module-scope Set.

Context:
- RFC defines operators: var (data access), ==, !=, >, <, >=, <= (comparison),
  and, or, ! (logical)
- Types already exist: SDUIRule, SDUIComparisonRule, SDUILogicalRule,
  SDUIVarOperator, SDUIConditional (in src/services/sdui/models/index.ts)
- Needs a function: evaluateRule(rule: SDUIRule, context: SDUIContext): boolean
- var operator uses resolveContextPath from interpolation.ts
- Invalid/malformed rules should return false (RFC 6.4: "Regras inválidas
  retornam false")
- For `visible` conditionals specifically: invalid rules should default to
  true (show component) — hiding content by default on bad data is worse UX
- Must be pure function, no side effects
- Reference: https://jsonlogic.com/

Deliverable: src/services/sdui/utils/jsonLogic.ts with evaluateRule() +
comprehensive unit tests covering all operators and edge cases.

      visibility and button enabled state.
  > ScreenRenderer.tsx: added isVisible() check using evaluateRule() — components
  >   with `visible: { rule: ... }` are hidden when rule evaluates to false.
  >   Malformed visible rules fail open (show component) for safe UX.
  > ButtonAdapter.tsx: added `enabled?: SDUIConditional` prop — evaluateRule()
  >   computes isEnabled, combined with disabled: `isDisabled = !!disabled || !isEnabled`.
  > ScreenRenderer.tsx: added separateConditionals() to extract SDUIConditional
  >   objects from props BEFORE interpolateDeep runs, preventing rule corruption.
  > 12 new unit tests (8 ScreenRenderer + 4 ButtonAdapter) — all 28 tests passing.
  >   ButtonAdapter tests assert actual disabled state via accessibilityState mock.
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIConditionals.stories.tsx with interactive

  >   sdui-conditionals-1500pts.png (button disabled, both alerts),

  >   validation returns null (valid). Skips entries with missing rule or message.
  > 10 unit tests passing (validation.test.ts): all-pass, first-fail, compound

  > PointsInputAdapter.tsx: integrated validation prop. buildValidationContext()
  >   merges current input value into context at contextKey path (supports nested
  >   paths like "form.points"). Validates on every onChange after first character
  >   (DS InputBox lacks onBlur prop). Clears error when input is emptied.
  > Storybook demo at src/__stories__/SDUIValidation.stories.tsx: balance toggle

Context:
